key_insight: Acoustoelastic Imaging reconstructs internal stress fields directly from shear wave propagation, turning stress imaging into a physics-informed inverse wave problem.
engineering_relevance: This provides a non-destructive way to map stress magnitude and principal directions in heterogeneous solids without requiring explicit constitutive models or material calibration.
method: Measure full-field shear waveforms in the stressed material.
Use full waveform inversion with physics-informed learning to recover wave-equation coefficients.
Convert recovered coefficients into stress magnitude and principal directions.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical simulations and ultrasound shear wave elastography experiments with sub-wavelength spatial resolution below 0.28λ and accurate reconstruction of nonuniform stress fields.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality and coverage of shear-wave measurements, and real materials or tissues could introduce noise, heterogeneity, or model mismatch that complicate inversion.
paper_title: Direct stress imaging from shear wave propagation
